text: Most of the camera works exactly the same as any other... there's a lens that focuses and a shutter that is normally closed, blocking light from passing through the camera, but opens when you want to take a picture. In a film camera, the light that gets through the shutter while it is open hits film, which is chemically sensitive to light. Digital cameras use a charged-couple device instead. This is a photosensitive (sensitive to light) computer chip, which converts the light energy into information that can be read by a computer.

text:
Intrinsic value is the value of a thing (or person) based on its internal value. It is the value per se. The thing has value in and of itself. The value is inherent (naturally in) the thing.

A forest has value for timber, mineral rights, tourism dollars; a shortcut to Grandmas house… but it also is a beautiful thing, perhaps a national treasure. The forest also has intrinsic value.

Then there are also the answers cited. Like Nike, the Swoosh is part of Nike’s intrinsic value. If they sold off the swoosh, the intrinsic value of Nike would go down.
adv_text:
Intrinsic value is the value of a thing (or person) based on its internal value. It is the value per se. The thing has value in and of itself. The value is inherent (naturally in) the thing.

A forest has value for timber, mineral rights, tourism dollars; a shortcut to Grandmas house… but it also is a beautiful thing, perhaps a national treasure. To forest also ha intrinsic value.

Then there are equally the answers cited. Like Nike, the Swoosh is part of Nike’s intrinsic value. If they sold off the swoosh, the intrinsic value of Nike would go down.

text: Octavian was the great nephew and adopted son of Julius Caesar. After the assassination of Caesar, Octavian and Mark Antony shared power. text: I would say the biggest disadvantage of an e-business is the lack of product-in-hand experience.  Because you cannot physically present the product to the customer, you have to prove its value through detailed descriptions and photos or fancy marketing. http://www.webopedia.com/

 
Short for RDF Site Summary or Rich Site Summary, an XML format for syndicating Web content. A Web site that wants to allow other sites to publish some of its content creates an RSS document and registers the document with an RSS publisher. A user that can read RSS-distributed content can use the content on a different site. Syndicated content includes such data as news feeds, events listings, news stories, headlines, project updates, excerpts from discussion forums or even corporate information. 
RSS was originally developed by Netscape.

To answer this question, I'm going to assume the Earth is a perfect sphere.

The Earth's average radius is about 6.37*10^6 m. Then you can use trignometry to figure out the radius at 38 degrees north.

cos(38 degrees)=r/6.38*10^6 m
r is about 5.03*10^6 m
Therefore the diameter is about 1.01*10^7 meters.

The Jehovah's Witnesses do not celebrate religious holidays because they have a strict reading of certain passages of the Bible.  For example, they do not celebrate Christmas, because the Bible does not command Christians to celebrate Christ's birth, but rather his death (Luke 22:19, 20).  There is no mention in the bible of early Christians celebrating Christmas or Easter.

Jehovahs don't celebrate birthdays, again because the only mention of birthday celebrations in the Bible is by non-Christians (Genesis 40:20-22; Mark 6:21, 22, 24-27)

The one religious holiday which Jehovahs do celebrate is the anniversary of Christ's death.

text: Enzyme levels are frequently measured in blood samples to determine if a person has had a heart attack.  Normally these enzymes are not present in the blood, but they are released into the blood stream as cardiac (heart) cells die as a result of the heart attack.  The cell bursts when it dies and spills the enzymes into the blood stream.  So when these enzymes are present, it means there has been cardiac cell death, and therefore a heart attack.

The Huns were a group of Central Asian nomadic tribes, who appeared in Europe in the 4th century. It has also become a more general term for any number of Central Asian equestrian nomads or semi-nomads. Most of these peoples are recorded by neighboring peoples to the south, east, and west as having occupied Central Asia roughly from the late 1st century to the mid-5th century.

Their possible Successor Nations are:
Bulgars 
Magyars 
Khazars 
Bashkir 
North Caucasian Huns 
Onogurs 
Various other Turkic peoples 
Mongolia 
Silla

text:
Partial pressure is a way of adding up pressures of gasses in Chemistry.

Using the Ideal Gas Law (PV = nRT), one can find the pressures of, say, three different containers.  Each of these pressures is called a partial pressure.

To find the theoretical total pressure (pretending that all 3 gasses in the 3 containers were mixed into one container, whose volume is identical to the three added up), you add up the partial pressures.

The "Date:" part of the message header does come from the email client. If you're using a web service to send email (like Hotmail or Yahoo mail) it will most likely reflect the time at the server itself and not from your pc.  (This may be where you're getting vastly different times reflected.)

If you're using an SMTP/POP service through Outlook or other non-web mail client, you should get something approximating your local pc time with a time zone indicator after the timestamp, like -0500 for U.S. Eastern, etc.

Hope that helps.

text:
Kingdom: Bacteria
Division: Cyanobacteria
The cyanobacteria were traditionally classified by morphology into five sections, referred to by the numerals I-V. The first three - Chroococcales, Pleurocapsales, and Oscillatoriales - are not supported by phylogenetic studies. However, the latter two - Nostocales and Stigonematales - are monophyletic, and make up the heterocystous cyanobacteria.

Most taxa included in the phylum or division Cyanobacteria have not been validly published under the Bacteriological Code. Except:

    * The classes Chroobacteria, Hormogoneae and Gloeobacteria

adv_text:
Kingdom: Bacteria
Division: Cyanobacteria
The cyanobacteria were traditionally classified by morphology into five sections, referred to by the numerals I-V. The first three - Chroococcales, Pleurocapsales, and Oscillatoriales - are not supported by phylogenetic studies. However, the latter two - Nostocales and Stigonematales - are monophyletic, and make up the heterocystous cyanobacteria.

Most taxa included in the phylum or division Cyanobacteria have not been validly published under the Bacteriological Code. Except:

    * The classes Chroobacteria, Hormogoneae and Gloeobacteria
    * The edict Chroococcales, Gloeobacterales, Nostocales, Oscillatoriales, Pleurocapsales and Stigonematales

text:
Shin splints usually come about as a result of over-training, coupled with insufficient rest and recovery. Do you run a lot (not sure 'cos you posted this under "volleyball")?

If you have recently upped the intensity or frequency of your running workouts, the increased wear-and-tear could contribute to shin splints.  Try to scale back a bit and give yourself adequate recovery time (e.g. at least one rest day between workouts if you're not used to running regularly).

In addition, you could try stretching your calf muscles more and running on softer surfaces (e.g. grass instead of the road) to lessen the impact.

Different times, different leaders, different priorities.

text:
"Clair de Lune" (Moonlight in French) is the name of various works in various fields of the arts.

In French, it is spelled without capitalizing the "L" in lune.

    * a poem by Paul Verlaine,
    * Claude Debussy's depiction of the poem in his Suite bergamasque (mostly spelled as "Claire de Lune"), and
    * Gabriel Fauré's setting of the lyrics.
    * a 1921 Broadway play written by Blanche Oelrichs for her husband John Barrymore to star in.
    * a 1932 French film directed by Henri Diamant-Berger based on the Oelrichs play.

first. ALWAYS start with the inner parentheses and work outward. 

So removing the parentheses we get:

-30x+9 + 30x-30 = x -7

We can combine the x's on the left side and the numbers. -30x + 30x is zero, so:

-21 = x - 7

We need to get rid of the -7, so we add 7 to both sides

-21+7 = x -7 + 7

so -14 = x

text:
Tennantite is a copper arsenic sulfosalt mineral. Its chemical formula is Cu12As4S13. It is found in hydrothermal veins and contact metamorphic deposits. A closely related mineral, tetrahedrite (Cu12Sb4S13) has antimony substituting for arsenic and the two form a solid solution series. The two have very similar properties and is often difficult to distinguish between tennantite and tetrahedrite. Iron, zinc, and silver substitute up to about 15% for the copper site.

The mineral was named after the English chemist Smithson Tennant (1761-1815).

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tetrahedrite

text:
Transmission losses are typically around 2-3% of electricity generated. Although relatively small, the electricity grid will be the single biggest user of electricity in most countries, above railways or major industrial plants.

Distribution will on average add another 4-5% of losses. In total, transmission and distribution losses will be around 7% (this figure applies to Europe as well as USA).

Losses vary a lot between countries. Germany, Finland have only 4%, but in developing countries, they can be well above 10% - see table 1.1 p 11 in below report.

text:
The integration by parts formula is
int(u*dv)=u*v-int(v*du)

Let u=r and dv=e^(r/2)dr

Now take the derivative of u with respect to r:
du=dr

Now integrate dv
int(dv)=int[e^(r/2)dr]
v=[e^(r/2)]/(1/2)  --> The chain rule was used here. 
__=2e^(r/2)
When you take the derivative you multiply by "what's inside" so when you integrate, you have to divide by it.

Now plug into the formula
2re^(r/2)-int(2e^(r/2)dr)

=2re^(r/2)-2(e^(r/2))/(1/2)+C

=2re^(r/2)-4(e^(r/2))+C

=(r-2)*2e^(r/2)+C

chkdsk /f

text: Leucism?\n\n"Leucism is characterized by reduced pigmentation, resulting in an individual with white or brighter patches of fur, plumage or skin than in the typical representative of the species. The phenotype may be inherited, or due to a mutation during the individual's developmental phase, but in many cases it is due to lack of proper nutrients. Some types of leucism are called partial albinism. F.e. some urban crows[1] exhibit this phenotype. The variations in feral dove's plumage exhibit the hereditary traits."

text:
A reservation is when you call and have a table set aside for you.

Call-ahead seating is when you call before you leave for the restaurant and put your name on the waiting list, or inquire as to whether you'd be able to be seated if you came. 

In other words, a reservation is (ideally) a sure thing; call-ahead seating just increases the chances that you'll be seated faster, or, if the line isn't long, confirms that you'd be able to get seated when you arrive.

text:
A normal Hydrogen atom has one proton and one electron.  If you strip away the electron, you are left with the Hydrogen +1 ion, which is the same thing as a proton.

There are two other Hydrogen isotopes (much MUCH less common than Hydrogen-1), there are Hydrogen-2 (1 proton, 1 neutron, 1 electron), and Hydrogen-3 (1 proton, 2 neutron, 1 electron).
If you were to strip away their electrons, they would still be the a Hydrogen +1 ion, but would be heavier since they have an extra 1 or two neutrons.

You should start by reading - reading a lot.

Then by paper trading.

text: I've been on light therapy - it's usually used for SAD - Seasonal Adjusted Depression - it's for winter blues that come from having too little light during the day, and then we spend most of the day inside and aren't exposed to actual sunlight. If your problem is "winter blues" then, yes, light therapy does work.

text:
Based on one of my chem classes:

A reaction goes to completion if:
>there's a precipitate.
>one of the product is a gas and the system isn't isolated.
>conditions are kept constant.

I think the 'precipitation' you are asking is a 'precipitation reaction'. This effect is useful in many industrial and scientific applications whereby a chemical reaction may produce a solid that can be collected from the solution by various methods (e.g. filtration, decanting, centrifuging). Precipitation from a solid solution is also a useful way to strengthen alloys.

text:
Flush: (1) A redness of the skin, typically over the cheeks or neck. A flush is usually temporary and brought on by excitement, exercise, fever, or embarrassment. Flushing is an involuntary (uncontrollable) response of the nervous system leading to widening of the capillaries of the involved skin. Also referred to as a blush (or, as a verb, to blush). Flushing may also be caused by medications or other substances that cause widening of the capillaries, such as niacin. (2) Flush also means to wash out a wound or body area.

I hope this helps...

Don

text: A venture capital firm manages (invests) funds on behalf of limited partners (LP's). Limited partners are typically either university endowments, state and local pension funds, or high-net worth individuals. Once raised, each fund is then invested into small entrepreneurial companies from which a large multiple is expected to be returned through an Initial Public Offering (IPO) or an acqusition. Once an entire fund is returned, the fund is then distributed back to the LP's at the same rate at which they invested.

One way to find the percentage of a value is to convert the percentage into a number then multiply the value by this number.
You convert a percentage into a number by dividing the percentage by 100: x% = x/100.

For example: find 5% of 200.
1) Convert the percentage to a number:
5% = 5/100 = 0.05
2) Multiply the value by the number:
200*0.05=10

In your case,
1) Convert the percentage to a number:
91&3/8% = 91.375/100 = 0.91375
2) Multiply the value by the number:
1000*0.91375 = 913.75

You would have $913.75

text: I work in the Financial Aid Office at Northwestern University. I've seen many of these such letters, and as far as I know there really is no set format. text:
Fats are basicly long chains of carbon and hydrogen atoms.  Each carbon atom can bond with up to four other atoms because of how many electrons they have in their outer shells.

In a saturated fat, each carbon molecule is attached to two other carbons and two hyrdrogen.

In a mono-unsaturated fat, ONE pair of carbon atoms are double bonded, meaning that they are both attached to two OTHER carbon atoms, one hyrogen atom, and to eachother TWICE.

In a polyunsaturated, there are more than one pair of carbon atoms double bonded.

Good luck.

text: Hyperthyroidism is any condition that results in too much thyroid hormone being produced by the thyroid gland.  Grave's disease is one cause of this condition where the entire gland becomes "overactive" and produces too much thyroid hormone.  There are other conditions that cause hyperthyroidism, but the mechnism and nature of these conditions are different from Grave's disease.  Tumors of the thyroid, and sometimes an inflammation of the thyroid can also cause hyperthyroidism.  Your doctor can do tests to determine the cause.  So in short, Grave's disease is not synonymous with hyperthyroidism, but it is one cause of it.

text:
Generally speaking, the symptoms of Conn's syndrome are:
High blood pressure
Muscle cramps
Headaches
Metabolic alkalosis
Low calcium levels

Low calcium levels can lead to other problems and symptoms such as:
Cardiac arrest
Muscle contractions
Carpopedal spasm 

Extreme weakness and tiredness are not normal symptoms of Conn's syndrome, or hypertension.  However, they are symptoms of stress and depression.  I'm no medical expert, but I would see a physician as soon as possible. Perhaps even get a second opinion.

text: Nondisjunction occurs during meiosis (cell division that produces gametes), specifically anaphase I. Normally, homologous chromosome pairs are lined up in the middle during metaphase I and are seperated from one another in anaphase I, a process called disjunction. However when this process does not occur, both chromosomes in the pair are carried together to the same pole of the cell, resulting in one cell having too many chromosomes and the other having too few. This can lead to genetic disorders, such as Down's syndrome (aka trisomy 21 - 3 copies of chromosome 21). Hope this helps!

text: ANGLO-SAXON CULTURE\n\nFighting was a way of life, and not to avenge the death of a family member was a social disgrace, so endlessly intricate blood-feuds generated perpetual excuses for going to war. The two alternatives for ending a blood-feud were 1) paying "wergild" -- the man price, or 2) arranging a marriage. Women were known as "cup-bearers" (because they served the mead) and "peace-weavers" (because of this function whereby feuds could be ended). But none of this really ever worked. The germanic tribes hated peace; fighting was more honorable.

text: Think of this as a ratio: When you use 1929KWH, the bill is $214.62. First, change $214.62 into 21,462 cents in order to consistant with units. Now in order to change 21,462 into 1, you must divide it by 21,462. Back to the ratio, divide both the numerator and the denomerator by 21,642, and get ~ 0.00898797 KWH to a cent. Because you want to flip this, we do 1/(0.00898797KWH/cent) ~ 11.125928 cents per KWH. Then due to significant figures we round this off to a bit more than 11 cents a KWH.
